# Meta data

<pre>
Ramon benchmark for Z3
-
Job description: QF_LIA, lia_w branch, default options, seed 1
Job tag: lia_w_vs_master_qflia_20_rs_1
Runner: rise-runner-2
Z3 repo: Z3Prover/z3
Z3 commit: 373398e81b3e1ee33a910e91a7d5091d0559149b
Z3 branch: lia_w
Z3 options: "-T:20 smt.random_seed=1"
Z3 inputs: https://zenodo.org/records/16740866/files/QF_LIA.tar.zst?download=1
Z3 commit message: lp: gate Gomory-with-dio on consecutive dio failures; separate config from runtime

The Diophantine controller used a single field both as the static
lp.dio_cuts_enable_gomory parameter and as the live runtime flag for
whether Gomory is running, and it started running Gomory once the
(randomization-inflated) dio_calls_period crossed a hard-coded 16. On
instances where dio is only intermittently productive the random gate
could ratchet the period past 16 by chance and turn Gomory (plus the
gcd test) on, thrashing.

This change:
- splits the shared field into m_dio_cuts_enable_gomory (static config,
  never mutated) and m_run_gomory_with_dio (runtime flag), so toggling
  the runtime state never clobbers the user's parameter;
- triggers running Gomory on a count of consecutive genuine dio undef
  returns (reset on a dio conflict) instead of the period proxy, which
  is robust to random_hammers gate variance;
- adds lp.dio_gomory_enable_period (default 16) to make that threshold
  configurable; set it very large to never auto-start Gomory so it
  follows dio_cuts_enable_gomory only;
- removes the debug-only dio_calls_period_trace parameter and the
  per-hammer / period-evolution verbose tracing used during analysis.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

</pre>
